item.title: Can Ubisoft just go down already?
item.dc:creator: Raoul97533

item.title: A buncha my doodles!
item.description:
Starting with an obligatory selfie post - notice the random Etch A Sketch stack on the ground. They propagate throughout every office I've ever had. I'm mostly known 'round these parts for my Etch A Sketch stuff, but I'm actually really really into doodling too! I hope you enjoy scrolling through some select favorites I done did doodled over the years.
My newest doodle! This probably took me around 6-8 hours. A point of pride for this doodle is that I worked on it almost exclusively during local craft meetups! It's been a goal of mine for years to get out and art with people IRL more, so this doodle serves as a testament to the mission accomplished.
Surprisingly enough, this is one of my largest doodles - I made this back in 2013. Note to self: explore more paint marker brands. I'd love to hear your suggestions if you have any!
This doodle was made early in the pandemic, back when I had wacky thoughts of making downloadable coloring book pages. They never flew off the shelves like I was hoping (I guess people don't wanna color viruses!) but I always loved how this felt more like a technical, medical type of illustration.
It's not often that I incorporate recognizable elements in my art. I'm still quite fond of this rendition and feel like the representational elements really mingled well with one another, even if they were all rendered as a part of a stream of consciousness.
This is the tiniest doodle I've ever doodled! If I were to wager a guess for how long this took, the answer is yes. (probably around 20 hours?).
This one time in college I made a jellyfish out of jellyfish!
I even made an umbrella out of it! The little jellyfish at the end were made from easter egg bottoms. If I could turn back time, there are two things in life I would do differently regarding this project: 1. I would trim my bangs 2. I would not forget the umbrella in a McDonald's where it is then stolen (thankfully I dismantled the jellyfish components from it, but that was an EXPENSIVE UMBRELLA and I'm still mad about it)
I feel like this doodle looks really good when you look at it from far away - a quality I rarely think about with my doodle work.
As a final post, here is a one-line Etch A Sketch stream of consciousness doodle (to tie it all together). I started doodling in 2009 and have made so many, so it's hard to pick favorites. I hope you enjoyed the small selection I chose to share today!
item.dc:creator: pikajane

item.title: I have too many memes: Dump #102
item.description: Unfortunately Elongated Muskrat kind of proves this wrong
item.dc:creator: Sweenerd

item.title: Punk Rock Museum(Las Vegas, NV) highlights
item.description:
As a trans woman, this def put me in my feels. I needed to hear this 30 years ago so that’s why even though I’m terrified, visibility is so important for the next generation. Most Viral edit:Being kind is punk af. In an age of division and vitriol, look for the helpers.
Would this be deserving of the selfie ban? Will remove if necessary, just love those jackets.
I’ve seen Gogol Bordello 10 times and they have never had a bad show.
I GOT TO PLAY EUGENE HUTZ’S GUITAR!? I’m still squealing from that excitement.
Ladies Room ‘art’.
The irony/self awareness of ‘no stickers on the glass’ is hilarious to me.
No need to go to Paris!
item.dc:creator: DaymanAhAAAAah

item.title: Stolen Movie Details! :)
item.description:
In Dune Part Two (2024) I would fall for the whole Lisan Al Gaib thing because I’d believe anything Rebecca Ferguson says tells me. This is a reference to me having a major crush on Rebecca Ferguson and also being easily manipulated
In The Breakfast Club, We're supposed to believe that this girl is ugly.
Were Watchmen (2019) produced today, this scene would have been cut from the storyboards for budget reasons
In Materialists (2025), Dakota Johnson orders a bottle of Coca-Cola. It's a pretty clever reference to the fact that she struggled to open a Pepsi can for the entirety of Madam Web (2024)
In Interstellar (2014) the physicist shows absolutely no emotions when finally meeting his colleagues after waiting alone for 23 years on an isolated spaceship like it’s a common thing to do
In Troy (2004) allegiances would remain forged and countless lives would have been spared if Orlando Bloom wasn't horny.
Before playing Joker Joaquin Phoenix also played The Batman
Another age-fail with Joaquin Phoenix. Joker and Bruce Wayne should be around the same age. So why the fuck is Arthur 30 years older in Joker?
The Nutty Professor (1996) and The Substance (2024) are basically the same movie but The Nutty Professor has a moral and wholesome ending
Vampire Diaries (2009 - 2017) is about 150 year old, fully emotionally and physically mature men seducing 17 year old girls.
In “Austin Powers: Goldmember” (2002) it is revealed that Austin Powers and Dr. Evil are twin brothers. This is a reference to their actors looking somewhat similar.
Despite $2.2b in Box Office Returns, "Titanic"(1997) failed to take off as a franchise.
Can we please appreciate the fact that at this point in Star wars: the Phantom menace (1999) Darth Maul has no idea who anakin is or his importance to the jedi, but still thinks its a fun idea to run over a random kid in the desert with his motorized vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Luvlyquants

item.title: What Happened Today #48
item.description: ? 20 stories. One day. Democracy under siege. Trump escalates attacks on courts, immigrants, and public institutions. The ICC is crumbling, DHS is detaining academics, and Musk-backed lawmakers push to remove judges. Meanwhile, protests grow as Tesla stock tanks. We’ve never had to cover this much in one day—because it’s never been this relentless. ⚡ Stay informed. Stay engaged. ? Subscribe free → https://govbrief.today ? Share this. Keep the spotlight on. #GovBriefToday #Resist2025 1. https://www.theguardian.com/world/2025/mar/20/ukraine-war-briefing-trump-wants-zaporizhzhia-power-plant-for-us 2. https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/education/2025/03/19/trump-eliminate-education-department-order/82235735007/ 3. https://www.nhonews.com/news/pentagon-blames-mistake-for-deletion-of-navajo-code-talkers-pages-in-dei-scrub-says-content/article_9253c3ef-ca95-4769-b853-0e5e43a68755.html 4. https://abcnews.go.com/Business/fed-set-make-interest-rate-decision-outbreak-trade/story?id=119905993 5. https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/us/judge-orders-trump-administration-to-return-two-transgender-inmates-to-women-s-prisons/ar-AA1Bg9AA?ocid=TobArticle 6. https://abcnews.go.com/US/wireStory/justice-department-moves-drop-lawsuit-texas-police-arrest-119962096 7. https://thehill.com/homenews/administration/5203401-elonauskidagency/ 8. https://www.courthousenews.com/texas-senate-passes-bill-requiring-ten-commandments-in-schools/ 9. https://apnews.com/article/social-security-offices-closures-doge-trump-b2b1a5b2ba4fb968abc3379bf90715ff 10. https://www.propublica.org/article/geo-group-ice-detainees-wage 11. https://www.cbsnews.com/news/judge-denies-bid-stop-doge-takeover-u-s-institute-of-peace/ 12. https://www.mediaite.com/news/former-trump-attorneys-social-security-number-revealed-in-jfk-files-dump-its-absolutely-outrageous/ 13. https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/mar/19/trump-new-mexico-militarized-border 14. https://www.politico.com/news/2025/03/19/trump-deportation-georgetown-graduate-student-00239754 15. https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c8712p5y48do 16. https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/trump-administration/trump-administration-pushes-back-judges-request-answers-deporation-fli-rcna197050-boasberg-justice-department-tren-de-aragua-el-salvador 17. https://www.nytimes.com/2025/03/19/world/middleeast/trump-houthis-yemen-strikes.html 18. https://www.msn.com/en-ca/news/world/musk-donates-thousands-to-gop-members-of-congress-pushing-to-impeach-judge-who-oppose-trump-report/ar-AA1BgOQB 19. https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-03-19/war-crimes-court-says-trump-sanctions-put-its-existence-at-risk?srnd=undefined 20. https://newrepublic.com/post/192946/french-scientist-denied-us-entry-trump-criticism 21. https://www.theverge.com/news/633091/tesla-takedown-protesters-planning-biggest-day-of-action
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: Supreme irony that a Japanese diplomat was saving Jews from the Nazis, while a German businessman in China saved many Chinese from being massacred by the Japanese.
item.description: Chiune Sugihara, often referred to as the "Japanese Schindler," was a diplomat stationed in Kaunas, Lithuania, during the tumultuous period of World War II. In 1940, as Nazi Germany advanced and the Soviet Union annexed Lithuania, thousands of Jewish refugees found themselves trapped, unable to escape the horrors of the Holocaust. Sugihara, serving as the Japanese vice-consul, was approached by desperate Jewish families seeking transit visas to escape through Japan to safer destinations. Despite strict orders from his government to deny such requests, Sugihara chose to act on his conscience. Over a span of weeks, he tirelessly issued transit visas, often working up to 20 hours a day, even as his consulate was ordered to close. His dedication was so profound that he continued signing visas from the window of a departing train, throwing them into the crowd of refugees as he left Lithuania. Sugihara's actions saved over 6,000 Jewish lives, as the visas he issued allowed families to travel through Japan and find refuge in other countries. His bravery came at great personal cost—he was dismissed from his diplomatic career after the war and lived in relative obscurity for years. It wasn't until decades later that his heroic deeds were widely recognized. In 1985, Israel honored him as "Righteous Among the Nations," a title given to non-Jews who risked their lives to save Jews during the Holocaust. Today, Sugihara's legacy endures as a testament to the power of individual courage and moral conviction in the face of unimaginable adversity. His story reminds us that even in the darkest times, one person's actions can illuminate a path to hope and survival.
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.title: "The lifts were out, and I'm not carrying it down the stairs"
item.dc:creator: SandettieLightVesselAutomatic

item.title: Armored Ciri Cosplay by LittleLuckyCosplay | Photography by World of Gwendana
item.description:
Armored Ciri Cosplay based on Witcher 3 concept art by Klaus Wittmann. Everything handmade by me out of EVA foam/fake leather/3D printing. Printed Aerondight because I’ve made Zireael in the past and wanted to mix it up (the spikes on the hand guard also matched the studs on her gloves in the concept art). I owe my life to the incredibly talented photographer @WorldofGwendana for these shots. I like to think I have a good amount of experience in making cosplay; however, I have close to 0 experience in posing for photographs, as I’ve been rocking iPhone photography for the majority of the time that I’ve cosplayed. I learned so much during this shoot and received so much guidance and direction - I could not have achieved pictures anywhere near this level of badassery without World of Gwendana’s expertise. His portfolio is incredible and I am so honored to now be among the subjects. It was an absolute pleasure and I cannot stop staring at these photos, so happy to pay homage to a character that I love so much!!
This might be one of my favorite shots!!!
Love that you can see the Witcher hook in the back (though it flipped around in this pic lol)
Angery
item.dc:creator: LittleLuckyCosplay

item.title: Australian Prime Minister gives an influencer a brilliant idea for her next video. :)

item.title: US Disappearing People Juxtaposed
item.description: The U.S. government is taking people off the street and sending them to the place in the bottom picture without charges filed, no arraignments, no due process and without notifying anyone of the people’s whereabouts. El Salvador intends to never release anyone from CECOT. #HumanRights #USpol #ElSalvador #CECOT #prison alt text: collage of two images top image shows many people in striped pajama-like clothing confined in small, stacked compartments, labeled 'Dachau, Germany - 1945' bottom image shows many people in white pajama-like clothing, also confined in small, stacked compartments, labeled 'CECOT, El Salvador - 2025.'
item.dc:creator: davidaugust

item.title: Thursday WHOLESOME DUMP :) Watch this is if you need your soul to smile #593
item.description:
1. "Son, I'm so proud right now!"
2. Arnold being asked to read famous movie lines in his own unique way!
3. Ahhh yes, a catfish in it's natural habitat
4. Doing a cute dance in between filming a scene
5. What emotional roller coaster of a video
6
7. Just a video of a trebuchet
8. Yo, can you fix my nails real quick too
9
10. Nature
11. The substitute professor came in to class today
12
13. Dog shows cat love
14. And finally something wholesome from myself! I've been working on this wacky narrative game called Do Not Press The Button and today I'm happy to announce that game will release next month - on 16th of April 2025! Please enjoy the new trailer I made to mark the occasion! And if you want to support small indie dev teams like me and my brother, please wishlist the game on Steam: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_Or_Youll_Delete_The_Multiverse/ Part 1
15. Part 2 of the trailer: Currently my favorite meme is the ABSOLUTE CINEMA. Please honestly tell me if I've earned it, with this trailer? I mean it's B.A.F.T.A.* nominated!
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames
